{{Quote|Notice that half of the swoopers are human, and there's not a single human on the Podracing pro-circuit. Coincidence?|Groff Zugga|<ref name="Holo">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|sports/13328_1.html|Podracing Faces Uncertain Future}}</ref>}}
The [[Unidentified four-breasted horned species|alien]] leader of the [[Galactic Spice Mining Guild]], '''Groff Zugga''' further served as a guild [[Boss/Legends|boss]] on the [[Spice/Legends|spice]] mining [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mon Gazza/Legends|Mon Gazza]] during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Under Zugga's leadership, the mines were considered second only to the operations located on the planet [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. From time to time, [[Gender/Legends|he]] hosted [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]] on the planet, including the [[Mon Gazza Speedway/Legends|Mon Gazza Speedway]], [[Spice Mine Run]], and the [[Zugga Challenge/Legends|Zugga Challenge]]. Years later during [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Zugga spoke to the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] regarding the hardships that the sport of Podracing was facing, and voiced resentment over the media's favoring of [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racing]], a sport primarily populated by [[Human/Legends|humans]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I see that our host—the guild boss himself—the infamous Groff Zugga is about to signal the start of the race.|[[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Beed Annodue]]|<ref name="Racer" />}}
[[File:GroffZugga-Ep1Racer.jpg|thumb|left|Groff Zugga prepares to begin a Podrace]]
A hulking [[Unidentified four-breasted horned species|alien]] scoundrel, Groff Zugga led the [[Galactic Spice Mining Guild]] during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The guild [[Boss/Legends|boss]] lorded over the [[Mine/Legends|mines]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mon Gazza/Legends|Mon Gazza]],<ref name="Prima" /> which were regarded as being second only to the mines on the planet [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. The [[Crime lord/Legends|kingpin]]<ref name="Nint">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]</ref> was also known for his affinity for the sport of [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]], which resulted in Zugga hosting Podraces<ref name="Prima" /> that had been constructed over the mines by local gamblers.<ref name="Nint" /> Eventually, Zugga commissioned a track of his own, the [[Zugga Challenge/Legends|Zugga Challenge]].<ref name="Prima" /> While commissioning the initial design, Zugga sought to make the track the toughest in the circuit to reflect his own tendencies. After its construction, the course was indeed regarded as the toughest racetrack in the sport,<ref name="Nint" /> but by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]<ref name="NEC1">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The racing season presented in the [[Star Wars video games|game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' must have taken place during this year due to the appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> the Zugga Challenge had been surpassed by tougher courses elsewhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Nint" /> Eventually, the [[Sneevel/Legends|Sneevel]] [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Boles Roor/Legends|Boles Roor]] took the title of the Zugga Challenge's crowd favorite.<ref name="Racer" />

By 32 BBY,<ref name="NEC1" /> Zugga hosted three of Mon Gazza's race events: the [[Mon Gazza Speedway/Legends|Mon Gazza Speedway]], [[Spice Mine Run]], and the Zugga Challenge.<ref name="Racer" /> Generally, Zugga would hang near the side of the track in his [[Repulsorlift chair/Legends|hoverchair]] and wave to the audience whilst staring down the Podracer [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]. However, by the time the race had begun, Zugga would have retreated to his private spectator booth in order to negotiate business with various individuals of dubious natures from across the galaxy. Zugga would still keep an eye on the race, however, with the use of a remote [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]].<ref name="Prima" />

Years later during [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="HNN">[[HoloNet News (original website)|''HoloNet News'' website]] "CoCo District Edition"</ref> Podracing—which was already considered a dubiously legal sport at best—came under media fire due to declining ticket sales and a rash of sudden scandals. In an interview with the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], Zugga compared [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racing]] with Podracing, noting that both sports shared an array of similarities with one glaring difference: the current Podracer roster lacked any notable [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Although not explicitly stated, Zugga inferred that the apparent passes given to swoop racing were of a xenophobic nature.<ref name="Holo" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|There's nothing bad you can say about Podracing that isn't also true of swoop racing, and swoopers are deity in the HoloNews.|Groff Zugga on swoop racing|<ref name="Holo" />}}
Groff Zugga belonged to an alien [[Species/Legends|species]] that featured four [[Breast/Legends|breasts]], skull-mounted [[Horn/Legends|horns]], and large four-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]] and three-toed feet. Zugga's arms were covered in [[Color/Legends|brown]]-colored [[Hair/Legends|hair]] which was complimented by orange and yellow skin and orange-colored [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The third toe on each of his feet featured an overgrown toenail.<ref name="Prima" />

Referred to as a notorious scoundrel, Zugga considered himself as a hardened individual. While commissioning the Zugga Challenge,<ref name="Prima" /> the brooding kingpin<ref name="Nint" /> wanted this trait visually defined by the course's toughness.<ref name="Prima" /> A staunch supporter of Podracing, Zugga was outraged over allegations of corruption in the sport, citing that the sport's primary rival, [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] racing, featured many of the same setbacks. Although unwilling to make direct implications, Zugga inferred that swoop racing was given a pass merely because the sport boasted a sizable roster of human competitors, as opposed to the lack of any humans in Podracing.<ref name="Holo" />

==Behind the scenes==
Groff Zugga was created for the [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'', which was released as a tie-in product for the film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. However, Zugga only physically appears in the game's home computer port, being entirely absent from the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo 64|Nintendo 64]] version of the game.<ref name="Racer" /> Zugga's backstory was further explained in two video game guides: ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]''<ref name="Prima" /> and [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]], with the latter being Zugga's only tie to the Nintendo 64 port of the game.<ref name="Nint" />
